Attestation
: A formal governance and compliance process that requires model owners to certify model attributes at specific points in time, reviewed by the second line of defense, typically the validator associated with the model. On the {{< var validmind.platform >}}, attestation captures snapshots of models and findings, preserving a fixed historical record. Attestation creates an audit trail and confirms that key controls, governance and documentation requirements have been met across the model’s lifecycle.

Attestation instance
: A specific occurrence of an attestation process created for model owners and reviewers when an execution schedule is triggered. Each instance contains a model snapshot, questionnaire responses, and review status, providing a complete record of the attestation review and approval process.

Attestation period
: A defined timeframe during which an attestation process is active, with specific start and end dates. Each attestation period creates a snapshot of model data that remains unchanged throughout the attestation process. Attestation periods are typically scheduled quarterly or annually and can be configured to align with regulatory or internal review cycles.

Attestation questionnaire
: A structured form that model owners complete to confirm model status, documentation, and governance compliance. The questionnaire can be configured to include rich text formatting, such as checkboxes or text fields. It serves as both a compliance check and a formal record of review.

Execution schedule
: The automated or manual triggering of an attestation process based on configured attestation periods. When executed, the schedule creates attestation instances, capturing model snapshots and initiating the review workflow.

Model snapshot
: A point-in-time capture of model data, including optional custom fields and associated findings, that remains fixed throughout the attestation process even if live data changes. This snapshot ensures the historical accuracy of the attestation record.
